---
title: "About Me"
page-layout: article
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/CodyGrad23fn-1-2.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/codymgeoffroy
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://linkedin.com/in/cody-geoffroy
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:geoffroycody@gmail.com
---

## Education

**Biotechnology, M.S. (incomplete)** <br>
University of Central Florida | 2024–2025

**Biomedical Sciences, B.S.** <br>
*Minor in Genomics & Bioinformatics | Minor in Anthropology* <br>
University of Central Florida | 2019–2023


## My Journey

My interest in bioinformatics emerged from a fascination with how computational
approaches can decode biological complexity. With a foundation in biomedical
sciences and genomics, I'm now focused on building strong programming and data
analysis skills to bridge wet-lab biology and computational research.

I'm working through bioinformatics problem sets, building analysis pipelines,
and documenting my learning journey through this portfolio. My background
includes teaching experience in gymnastics and education, which has strengthened
my ability to break down complex concepts and communicate effectively.

## Technical Skills

#### Programming Languages
- Python (primary focus for bioinformatics)
- R (statistical analysis, DESeq2, MOFA2, mixOmics)
- Bash/Shell scripting
- SQL for database querying

#### Bioinformatics & Multi-Omics
- Pharmacogenomic genotyping (PharmCAT, CYP2C19/CYP2D6 activity scoring)
- Differential expression analysis (DESeq2 with continuous covariates)
- Single-cell ATAC-seq (ArchR/Signac, chromVAR, MACS2)
- Multi-omics integration (MOFA2, DIABLO/mixOmics)
- Pathway enrichment (clusterProfiler: GO, KEGG, Reactome)

#### Machine Learning & Data Science
- XGBoost with SHAP interpretability
- PyTorch (VAE benchmarking)
- Optuna hyperparameter optimization
- LLM-based interpretation pipelines (RAG with PubMed)

#### DevOps & Reproducibility
- Docker containerization (Bioconductor + Python multi-stage builds)
- Snakemake workflow orchestration
- Git/GitHub version control + GitHub Actions CI
- Linux/WSL development environment
- Quarto for reproducible research and portfolio

## Interests Beyond Science
:::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### Scuba Diving
Open-water and nitrox certified. Diving demands methodical preparation,
situational awareness, and staying calm under pressure — skills that translate
directly to research and problem solving.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### Martial Arts
Black belt in Taekwondo, with training in Muay Thai and Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.
Martial arts taught me that mastery is a process, not a destination — a
philosophy I carry into learning bioinformatics.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### Yu-Gi-Oh
Competitive player focused on deck building and strategic play. The game
demands pattern recognition, probabilistic thinking, and adapting under
pressure.
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
#### Writing
Currently writing *Useless Man: Embracing the Exiled Self* — a philosophical
exploration of identity, purpose, and the freedom found in not fitting the
expected mold.
:::
::::


## What's Next

I'm actively seeking opportunities in bioinformatics, computational biology, or
data science where I can apply my growing technical skills and contribute to
meaningful research. I'm particularly interested in pharmacogenomics, multi-omics
integration, and data-driven biological discovery.

**Open to:** Full-time positions, internships, collaborations, and freelance projects

[Download Resume](files/Resume_CG.pdf){.btn .btn-primary} [Download CV](files/CV_CG.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-secondary}
